chapter_title.ar: بَابُ الْقَوْلِ عِنْدَ الْإِصْبَاحِ وَ الْإِمْسَاءِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 48 – The words (to be spoken) during the morning and the evening

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (12/224)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (12/224)
text: تَقُولُ إِذَا أَصْبَحْتَ أَصْبَحْتُ بِاللَّهِ مُؤْمِناً عَلَى دِينِ مُحَمَّدٍ وَ سُنَّتِهِ وَ دِينِ عَلِيٍّ وَ سُنَّتِهِ وَ دِينِ الْأَوْصِيَاءِ وَ سُنَّتِهِمْ آمَنْتُ بِسِرِّهِمْ وَ عَلَانِيَتِهِمْ وَ شَاهِدِهِمْ وَ غَائِبِهِمْ وَ أَعُوذُ بِاللَّهِ مِمَّا اسْتَعَاذَ مِنْهُ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) وَ عَلِيٌّ ( عليه السلام ) وَ الْأَوْصِيَاءُ وَ أَرْغَبُ إِلَى اللَّهِ فِيمَا رَغِبُوا إِلَيْهِ وَ لَا حَوْلَ وَ لَا قُوَّةَ إِلَّا بِاللَّهِ .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin yahya, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Al Hajjal and Bakr Bin Muhammad, from Abu Is’haq Al Shaeery, from Yazeed Bin Kalsamat,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup>, or from Abu Ja’far<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘When you wake up in the morning, do so as a believer in Allah<sup>azwj</sup>, upon the Religion of Muhammad<sup>saww</sup> and his<sup>saww</sup> Sunnah, and the Religion of Ali<sup>asws</sup> and his<sup>asws</sup> Sunnah, and the Religion of the successors<sup>asws</sup> and their<sup>asws</sup> Sunnah. Believe in their<sup>asws</sup> secrets, and their public (matters), and their seen (matters) and their unseen, and seek Refuge with Allah<sup>azwj</sup> from whatever Rasool-Allah<sup>saww</sup> sought Refuge from, and (so did) Ali<sup>asws</sup>, and the successors<sup>asws</sup>, and desire to Allah<sup>azwj</sup> regarding whatever they<sup>asws</sup> desired to Him<sup>azwj</sup>, and there is neither Might nor Strength except with Allah<sup>azwj</sup>’.
en.sarwar: 4. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from Hajjal and Bakr ibn Muhammad from abu Ishaq al-Sha’Iri from Yazid ibn Kalthamah from abu ‘Abd Allah or abu Ja’far (a.s.) who has said the following: “In the morning say, ‘This morning I believe in Allah in the religion of Muhammad and his tradition, Sunnah , the religion of Ali and his tradition, the religion of the successors (of the Holy Prophet from his family) and their tradition. I believe in their secrets and that which is public of them, those present of them as well as those absent. I seek refuge before Allah from everything against which the Messenger of Allah, Ali and the successors (of the Holy Prophet from his family) sought refuge. I am interested in everything that was of interest to them. There are no means and no power without the help of Allah.’”
